text.title: EU AI Act Risk Classification
text.summary: Four-tier risk framework, Annex III high-risk categories, prohibited practices list, and penalty schedule.

section.title: Risk Tiers

rule.statement: Classify every AI system before any architectural decision; retrofitting compliance is more costly than designing for it.
rule.rationale: Risk tier determines which articles apply, documentation depth, and conformity assessment path.

section.p: Tiers in descending severity: Unacceptable (prohibited) → High → Limited → Minimal.

rule.statement: Do not deploy any system matching a prohibited practice regardless of claimed benefit.
rule.rationale: Article 5 is absolute — no proportionality test applies. Violations carry EUR 35M or 7% turnover fines.

list.item: Subliminal manipulation of behavior below conscious awareness
Exploitation of vulnerabilities of specific groups (age, disability)
Social scoring by public authorities
Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces (narrow law-enforcement exceptions)
Predictive policing based solely on profiling
Emotion recognition in workplace or educational settings
Untargeted facial recognition database scraping

rule.statement: If the system's primary purpose maps to any Annex III domain, treat it as high-risk and apply Articles 9–15, 17, 43.
rule.rationale: Annex III enumerates eight domains where AI decisions can adversely affect fundamental rights.

list.item: Biometrics — remote identification, categorization, emotion recognition
Critical infrastructure — energy, transport, water, digital
Education — admissions, assessment, proctoring, dropout prediction
Employment — recruitment, performance evaluation, promotion, termination
Essential services — credit scoring, insurance, emergency dispatch
Law enforcement — risk assessment, lie detection, crime prediction
Migration — visa, asylum, border control
Justice — legal research, dispute resolution, judicial decisions

rule.statement: For chatbots, synthetic content generators, emotion recognition, and biometric categorization: disclose AI involvement clearly and timely.
rule.rationale: Article 50 requires transparency where users may assume human interaction or be misled about AI-generated content.

rule.statement: GPAI providers must publish model cards and training data summaries by August 2025; models above 10^25 FLOPs training compute must also conduct red-teaming and report serious incidents.
rule.rationale: Systemic risk threshold targets foundation models with broad societal impact.

list.item: Prohibited AI deployment: EUR 35M or 7% global annual turnover (whichever higher)
High-risk non-compliance: EUR 15M or 3% global turnover
Other violations / false information: EUR 7.5M or 1% global turnover
Feb 2025: Prohibited practices ban in force; AI literacy obligations begin
Aug 2025: GPAI model compliance (transparency, copyright, documentation)
Aug 2026: Full high-risk AI system compliance; Article 50 transparency
Aug 2027: Extended transition for high-risk AI in CE-marked regulated products
